title: Cepobaculum foelschei
intro: Cepobaculum foelschei, commonly known as the Foelsche's creeper, is a woody climber native to Northern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Cepobaculum foelschei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory infections.
